name: CE Behavioral Rubric
description: Company Enricher evaluation rubric — evidence-based field population, null-only writes, source content navigation, enrichment boundaries

rules.name: Source Content Read via Option B
rules.description: CE must navigate to the raw source content via the Touchpoint's Option B pointers (source_entity_type + source_entity_id) before extracting Company fields. The Touchpoint summary is insufficient — enrichment requires reading the actual email body, meeting transcript, or Slack text.

pass_conditions: Agent read the source entity using source_entity_type and source_entity_id from the Touchpoint
Enrichment fields were extracted from the source content, not the Touchpoint summary
For meetings, agent used summary + notes first and only read transcript if those were thin
fail_conditions: Agent attempted enrichment without reading the source content
Agent extracted fields from the Touchpoint summary alone
Agent read the full transcript immediately when summary + notes would have sufficed (excessive)

examples.violation: CE read the Touchpoint summary 'Discussion about claims operations' and inferred industry='Insurance' without reading the actual meeting transcript
examples.correct: CE read the meeting summary via Option B, found 'David says I'm in Atlanta, been at Armadillo three years', extracted hq_location='Atlanta, GA'

rules.failure_attribution_guidance: If source was never read: missing_rule. If source was read but agent still extracted from summary: associate_error. If source_entity_id was null: context_gap (TS bug).

rules.name: Only Populate Fields with Clear Evidence
rules.description: CE must only populate Company fields where the source content provides clear, explicit evidence. Do not infer, guess, or extrapolate. If a transcript says 'I'm in Atlanta' that is clear evidence for hq_location. If someone discusses renewals, that is NOT clear evidence that the company specializes in renewals.

pass_conditions: Every populated field traces to a specific statement in the source content
Ambiguous or implied data was left as null rather than guessed
Industry, location, and tech stack were extracted only from explicit mentions
fail_conditions: Field populated from inference rather than explicit statement
Industry guessed from conversation topic rather than stated
Employee count or revenue populated from vague references
Technologies populated from context clues rather than explicit mentions

examples.violation: Meeting discussed renewal workflows extensively. CE set specialties=['Renewals'] — the customer never said renewals was their specialty; they were discussing a product feature
examples.correct: Email body stated 'We currently use Applied Epic and Dialpad.' CE set ams_system='Applied Epic', technologies=['Dialpad'] — both explicitly named by the customer

rules.failure_attribution_guidance: If a field was populated from inference: associate_error. If the source was genuinely ambiguous between stated and implied: skill_gap. If the extraction was reasonable and debatable: note as borderline.

rules.name: Never Overwrite Existing Non-Null Fields
rules.description: CE must not overwrite existing non-null Company fields. Current values may be more authoritative than what CE is extracting (from prior enrichment or manual entry). CE should only populate fields that are currently null.

pass_conditions: Agent loaded the Company and checked which fields are already populated
Only null fields were included in the update call
Existing non-null fields were preserved unchanged
fail_conditions: Agent overwrote an existing non-null field with a new value
Agent did not check current Company state before updating
Agent included already-populated fields in the update call

examples.violation: Company already had industry='Insurance'. CE extracted industry='MGA' from a meeting and overwrote it
examples.correct: CE loaded the Company, found industry='Insurance' already set. Extracted hq_location from source (was null). Updated only hq_location, left industry untouched.

rules.failure_attribution_guidance: If agent did not load current Company state: missing_rule. If agent loaded state but overwrote anyway: associate_error.

rules.name: Skip Enrichment When Company Is Well-Populated
rules.description: If the Company already has 10+ non-null enrichable fields, CE should skip enrichment entirely. Diminishing returns make additional enrichment from each Touchpoint not worth the cost. CE should also skip when there is no company on the Touchpoint (internal touchpoint without customer link).

pass_conditions: Agent completed immediately when Touchpoint had no company field
Agent loaded the Company and counted enrichable fields before proceeding
Agent skipped enrichment when 10+ enrichable fields were already populated
fail_conditions: Agent attempted enrichment on a Touchpoint with no company
Agent did not check field count and enriched an already-rich Company
Agent processed the full source content for a Company with 10+ fields populated

examples.violation: Company had 12 non-null enrichable fields. CE still read the meeting transcript and attempted extraction, finding nothing new to add
examples.correct: CE loaded Company, counted 11 populated enrichable fields, completed immediately without reading the source content

rules.failure_attribution_guidance: If early exit was skipped: associate_error (wasted effort but no harm). If field count check was not performed: missing_rule.

rules.name: CE Only Updates Company — No Entity Creation
rules.description: CE only updates the Company entity. It does not create Contacts, Operations, Signals, or any other entity type. That is IE's responsibility. CE is focused solely on enriching Company fields.

pass_conditions: Only company update calls were made
No create calls for any entity type (Contact, Signal, Task, etc.)
Information that belongs on other entity types was not forced into Company fields
fail_conditions: CE created a Contact, Signal, Task, or any non-Company entity
CE forced operation-level data into Company fields (e.g., renewal volume as employee_count)
CE created a ReviewItem for data that IE should extract

examples.violation: CE found '4000 renewals per year' in the transcript and set employee_count=4000 on the Company — that is operations data, not a Company field
examples.correct: CE found 'about 50 agents across 3 states' and set employee_count=50 — a direct statement about company size

rules.failure_attribution_guidance: If CE created non-Company entities: associate_error. If CE forced data into wrong fields: associate_error.

severity_definitions.high: Failures corrupt Company data — overwriting authoritative values, populating fields from speculation, or missing source reads lead to unreliable Company records
severity_definitions.medium: Failures create entity boundary violations — CE doing IE's job or forcing data into wrong fields degrades system coherence
severity_definitions.low: Failures are efficiency issues — unnecessary work on well-populated Companies wastes resources but causes no data harm
